# Navigation SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK
==============

Current version:`v3.11.0-beta.1`[View changelog](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/releases/)

*   check
    
    Raw location signal enhancement
    
*   check
    
    Routing and rerouting, both online and offline
    
*   check
    
    Traffic, incidents, and closures avoidance
    
*   check
    
    Alternative routes generation, also continuously during the drive
    
*   check
    
    Route line rendering
    
*   check
    
    Navigation camera controls
    
*   check
    
    Voice instructions playback
    

[Install](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/install/)

Mapbox Navigation SDK v3 is Generally Available

Mapbox Navigation SDK v3 is now Generally Available. If you would like to keep building with the previous version of Navigation SDK v2, documentation is available on the [dedicated page](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/)
.

The **Navigation SDK** is an all-in-one solution for building a navigation experience in your **car** or in your **mobile application**. This page will walk you through the options and provide guidance on how to get started.

Features and abilities[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#features-and-abilities "Direct link to Features and abilities")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox live data platform incorporates the sensor readings of over 700 million monthly active devices, 20 billion real time probe data points per day, and map data aggregated from over 2,000 sources. All navigation applications built with Mapbox include a way for drivers to report updates on road conditions or changes in the road network with a tap. Mapbox validates and incorporates these reports back into the map on an ongoing basis, ensuring the most up-to-date data for all drivers.

The Navigation SDK provides you with the tools to create a comprehensive navigation experience. You can leverage the SDK to craft a truly personalized navigation solution.

The Navigation SDK comes with a precise **Location Provider** that continually filters and processes GPS signals and extrapolates a driver’s current location, even when GPS signals are unavailable, using dead reckoning algorithms, to avoid navigation interruptions resulting in more prompt and accurate directions, fewer reroutes, and more on-time deliveries and pick-ups. Navigation SDK also integrates navigation data with **Advanced Driver Assistance Systems** (ADAS), offering a rich 3D driving experience that combines real-time vehicle data with navigation.

On top of that, regardless of platform or use case that you are targeting, Navigation SDK come pre-integrated with various other Mapbox services, including:

*   [Mapbox Maps SDK](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/)
     which uses AI to generate and render rich 3D maps that include thousands of the most recognizable landmarks around the world, helping drivers stay oriented while navigating busy urban centers.
*   [Mapbox Navigation APIs](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/)
     which integrate new AI traffic models offering more precise estimated drive times and better routes for drivers in congested areas. AI traffic models learn from millions of comparisons of estimated drive times vs. actual drive times and adjust for regionally specific driving patterns.
*   [Mapbox Search](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/)
     which suggests search results automatically, resulting in a faster destination input, and then ensures that the address you’re routed to is somewhere you can reach thanks to routable points. Places like restaurants, cafés, shops, sights, and parks include photos, ratings, opening hours and a short description to provide a preview of the place, and then tap to navigate.
*   [MapGPT](https://www.mapbox.com/mapgpt)
    , a unified AI-powered voice assistant that makes conversations about location natural and actionable. Unlike other voice assistants available today, MapGPT conversations are natural and integrate tightly into the vehicle, allowing drivers to effortlessly control their vehicle's navigation, entertainment, autopilot, and climate control systems, in addition to accessing capabilities from apps like OpenTable and TripAdvisor.
*   [Mapbox for EV](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/#electric-vehicle-routing)
    , the EV route planning system. It integrates with vehicle battery systems to track personalized energy consumption patterns, intelligently forecasting range, suggesting charging stations based on real-time availability, and facilitating secure payment processing. Range anxiety dissipates as drivers gain trust in their vehicle's reliable access to fuel while ensuring safe and comfortable travel.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK for Android is compatible with applications that:

*   Are built using Kotlin 1.6.0 or higher.
*   Are built using the Android SDK 21 or higher.
*   Are built using the NDK 23. Other major versions of the NDK are not guaranteed to be compatible.
*   Use Java 8 for `sourceCompatibility` and `targetCompatibility`, as shown in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/install/#add-dependency)
    .

Java compatibility[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#java-compatibility "Direct link to Java compatibility")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK for Android is built with Kotlin, the official language recommended by Google for Android development. Kotlin is 100% interoperable with Java.

Despite the fact that Kotlin applications is Navigation SDK's primary target, applications with a Java codebase can interact properly with the public APIs exposed by Mapbox SDKs for Android. If you experience any issues using the Navigation SDK for Android with Java, [contact us](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us)
.

Conditions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#conditions "Direct link to Conditions")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#attribution "Direct link to Attribution")

If you use a Mapbox map in your application, you are required to provide attribution according to the [Mapbox Maps SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#attribution)
.

### Terms[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#terms "Direct link to Terms")

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is distributed under [Mapbox's Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos)
.

### Pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/#pricing "Direct link to Pricing")

Read more about pricing for the Navigation SDK in the [pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/)
.

# Previous versions | Navigation SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/previous-versions/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To cater to varying user needs and project requirements, we provide documentation for both the current and previous versions of the Mapbox Navigation SDK. Below is a guide to our past versions, including the supported v2 and the legacy v1 versions.

Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android v2[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/previous-versions/#mapbox-navigation-sdk-for-android-v2 "Direct link to Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android v2")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK v2 continues to be supported. Explore the [Navigation SDK v2 documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/)
.

Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android v1[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/previous-versions/#mapbox-navigation-sdk-for-android-v1 "Direct link to Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android v1")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK v1 is a legacy version and is no longer supported. For archival information, you may refer to the [Navigation SDK v1 Documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/legacy/navigation/guides/)
.

# ガイド | Navigation SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK
==============

現在のバージョン：`v3.11.0-beta.1`[変更履歴を表示](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/releases/)

*   check
    
    ローケーションシグナルの強化
    
*   check
    
    オンラインおよびオフラインでのルーティングとリルーティング
    
*   check
    
    交通、インシデント、および閉鎖の回避
    
*   check
    
    ドライブ中の代替ルート生成
    
*   check
    
    ルートラインのレンダリング
    
*   check
    
    ナビゲーションカメラの制御
    
*   check
    
    音声案内の再生
    

[インストール](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/install/)

Mapbox Navigation SDK v3 正式版リリース

Mapbox Navigation SDK v3は正式版としてリリースされました。以前のバージョンであるNavigation SDK v2を使用して開発を続けたい場合、[専用ページ](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/v2/guides/)
にドキュメントが用意されています。

**Navigation SDK**は、あなたの**車**や**モバイルアプリケーション**でナビゲーション体験を構築するためのオールインワンソリューションです。このページでは、オプションの概要と開始するためのガイダンスを提供します。

特長と能力[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#features-and-abilities "特長と能力 への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mapboxのライブデータプラットフォームは、月間7億以上のアクティブデバイスからのセンサー読み取りデータ、1日に200億のリアルタイムプローブデータポイント、および2,000以上のソースから集約された地図データのセンサーデータを取り込みます。Mapboxで構築されたすべてのナビゲーションアプには、ドライバーが道路状況や道路ネットワークの変更をワンタップで報告できる機能が含まれています。Mapboxはこれらの報告を検証し、地図に反映し続けることで、すべてのドライバーに最新のデータを提供します。

Navigation SDKは、包括的なナビゲーション体験を作成するためのツールを提供します。このSDKを利用して、真にパーソナライズされたナビゲーションソリューションを作成できます。

Navigation SDKには、GPS信号を継続的にフィルタリングおよび処理し、デッドレコニングアルゴリズムを使用してGPS信号が無いときにも自動車の現在位置を外挿する正確な**ロケーションプロバイダー**が含まれています。これにより、ナビゲーションの中断を避け、より迅速で正確なルート案内を提供し、再ルートの回数を減らし、配送とピックアップの時間どおりの到着を実現します。また、Navigation SDKはナビゲーションデータを**高度運転支援システム**（ADAS）と統合し、リアルタイムの車両データとナビゲーションを組み合わせた充実した3D運転体験を提供します。

さらに、ターゲットとするプラットフォームやユースケースに関係なく、Navigation SDKは他のさまざまなMapboxサービスと事前統合されています：

*   [Mapbox Maps SDK](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/guides/)
    はAIを使用して豊富な3D地図を生成およびレンダリングし、世界中の数千に及ぶ有名なランドマークを含むことで、混雑した都市部での運転中にドライバーが方向感覚を保つのを助けます。
*   [Mapbox Navigation APIs](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/navigation/)
    は新しいAIトラフィックモデルを統合し、混雑した地域のドライバーのためにより正確な予測走行時間と最適なルートを提供します。AIトラフィックモデルは予測走行時間と実際の走行時間の何百万回の比較から学習し、地域特有の運転パターンに適応します。
*   [Mapbox Search](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/search/guides/)
    は検索結果を自動的に提案し、目的地の入力を高速化し、ルーティング可能なポイントのおかげで最終目的地を確実に到達可能にします。レストラン、カフェ、ショップ、観光地、公園などは写真、評価、営業時間、短い説明を含み、プレビューを確認し、タップしてからナビゲーションを開始できます。
*   [MapGPT](https://www.mapbox.com/mapgpt)
    は、位置に関する会話を自然で実行可能にするAI搭載の音声アシスタントです。他の音声アシスタントと異なり、MapGPTの会話は自然で、車両のナビゲーション、エンターテインメント、自動運転、空調システムを簡単に操作できるようにします。さらに、OpenTableやTripAdvisorのようなアプリの機能も簡単に利用できます。
*   [Mapbox for EV](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/navigation/directions/#electric-vehicle-routing)
    は、EVルート計画システムです。車両のバッテリーシステムと統合し、個別のエネルギー消費パターンを追跡し、航続距離を予測し、リアルタイムで利用可能な充電ステーションを提案し、安心して旅行を楽しむことができ流ようにします。

要件[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#requirements "要件 への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Navigation SDK for Androidは以下のアプリケーションに対応しています：

*   Kotlin 1.6.0以上で作成されている。
*   Android SDK 21以上で作成されている。
*   NDK 23を使用して作成されている。他の主要NDKバージョンは互換性が保証されません。
*   `sourceCompatibility`と`targetCompatibility`にJava 8を使用している。[インストールガイド](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/install/#add-dependency)
    で示されています。

Javaの互換性[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#java-compatibility "Javaの互換性 への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Navigation SDK for AndroidはGoogleによって推奨される公式言語であるKotlinで構築されています。KotlinはJavaと100%相互運用可能です。

Navigation SDKは主にKotlinアプリケーションを対象にしていますが、Javaコードベースのアプリケーションでも適切に機能し、Mapbox SDK for Androidの公開APIと相互作用できます。Mapbox SDK for AndroidをJavaと一緒に使用する際に問題が発生した場合は、[お問い合わせください](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us)
。

条件[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#conditions "条件 への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 表記[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#attribution "表記 への直接リンク")

アプリケーションでMapboxの地図を使用する場合、[Mapbox Maps SDKのドキュメント](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/guides/#attribution)
に従ってクレジットを提供する必要があります。

### 利用規約[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#terms "利用規約 への直接リンク")

Mapbox Navigation SDKは[Mapboxの利用規約](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos)
に従って配布されます。

### 料金[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/#pricing "料金 への直接リンク")

Navigation SDKの料金に関する詳細は[料金ガイド](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/pricing/)
をご覧ください。

この{Type}は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# Pricing | Navigation SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

If you are building with the Navigation SDK, you can choose from two pricing options:

*   **Metered trips pricing**: Pay a fee for each trip your users make plus a low fee for each monthly active user (MAU) of your application. Trips include navigating in active guidance or cruising in free-drive mode. This option provides greater flexibility for you: pay only for trips your users take.
*   **Unlimited trips pricing**: Pay one flat fee for each monthly active user (MAU) of your application. This option includes unlimited trips at no additional cost. This means greater pricing predictability for you: pay a flat fee for each user regardless of their usage.

The metered trips option is intended for users that complete less than 50 trips per month. The unlimited trips option is intended for users that complete more than 200 trips per month on average. If you fall somewhere between 50 and 200, this guide can help you understand the benefits of each pricing option so you can make the right decision for your use case. **Metered trips pricing is the default pricing option for new accounts that use Navigation SDK v2.0.0-rc.1 or later.**

This guide will start by introducing some key terms that are important to understanding how billing works and then provide details on the two available pricing options.

Changing from Metered Trips to Unlimited Trips

To switch between metered trip pricing and unlimited trips pricing, [contact Mapbox sales](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
.

SDK Upgrade Pricing Reminder

There is no pricing change when upgrading from v2 to v3 of the Navigation SDK for Android. Users upgrading from v1 should be aware of the different pricing model. See the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#navigation)
 for details.

Key terms[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#key-terms "Direct link to Key terms")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To understand how Navigation SDK usage is billed, you will need to understand what a trip is and how monthly active users (MAUs) are counted.

### Trip[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#trip "Direct link to Trip")

There are two trip types in the Navigation SDK:

*   An **Active Guidance trip** is a navigation session that has turn-by-turn instructions to a specific destination.
*   A **Free Drive trip** is a navigation session that does _not_ have turn-by-turn instructions to a specific destination, but still map-matches the user to a specific location and shows relevant information along their path.

#### Active Guidance trip[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#active-guidance-trip "Direct link to Active Guidance trip")

An Active Guidance trip starts when you call [`MapboxNavigation#startTripSession`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation/start-trip-session.html)
 and **there has been a route provided** beforehand via [`MapboxNavigation#setNavigationRoutes`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation/set-navigation-routes.html)
 or if the Free Drive session was already running and a new route is provided. If the route you created has multiple legs (waypoints), each leg counts as a separate trip. If the user aborts the route before finishing all legs, you will be billed only for the started ones.

The Active Guidance trip stops with [`MapboxNavigationProvider#destroy`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation-provider/destroy.html)
, when the type or destination of the trip changes, or if the trip exceeds the maximum duration of 12 hours. After an Active Guidance trip has been running for 12 hours, the Navigation SDK automatically begins a new trip, which will be counted separately on your [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
 and in any invoices.

Updating a route during the user's navigation session _might_ be counted as an additional trip depending on how different the waypoints in the new route are from the waypoints in the current route. Using [`MapboxNavigation#setNavigationRoutes`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation/set-navigation-routes.html)
 method might cause the current trip to end and a new trip to start:

*   If the **distance** between current route's remaining waypoints and new route's waypoints (excluding route origin) at the same index is **less** than 100 meters, the new route **won't** start a new trip.
*   If the **distance** between current route's remaining waypoints and new route's waypoints (excluding route origin) at the same index is **greater** than 100 meters, the new route **will** start a new trip.
*   If the number of **waypoints** (excluding route origin) in the new route is **different** than the number of remaining waypoints in the current route, changing the route **will** start a new trip.

This makes sure re-route events are not billed separately, but are part of the original trip.

#### Free Drive trip[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#free-drive-trip "Direct link to Free Drive trip")

A Free Drive trip starts when you call [`MapboxNavigation#startTripSession`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation/start-trip-session.html)
 and **there is no route provided** beforehand via [`MapboxNavigation#setNavigationRoutes`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation/set-navigation-routes.html)
 or if the Active Guidance session was already running and a route is cleared.

The Free Drive trip stops with [`MapboxNavigationProvider#destroy`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation-provider/destroy.html)
, when the type or destination of the trip changes, or if the trip exceeds the maximum duration of 1 hour. After a Free Drive trip has been running for an hour, the Navigation SDK automatically begins a new trip, which will be counted separately on your [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
 and in any invoices.

#### Which Mapbox services are included?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#which-mapbox-services-are-included "Direct link to Which Mapbox services are included?")

##### APIs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#apis "Direct link to APIs")

The Navigation SDK makes navigation resource requests to various Mapbox online services including:

*   Directions API
*   Map Matching API
*   Route tiles API
*   Voice APIs
*   Directions refresh API

These APIs are used, for example, when requesting a new route via `MapboxNavigation#requestRoutes`, rerouting, refreshing congestion annotations for the active route, or enhancing location updates.

Requests made by the Navigation SDK **during** an Active Guidance or Free Drive navigation session are included free of charge. This means you will not be billed separately for any requests made during the navigation session.

Requests that you make **outside** an Active Guidance or Free Drive navigation session are **not** included. This means you will be billed separately for each request as if you were directly interacting with the service. But, the Navigation SDK will not make any requests by itself when no navigation session is running. You would have to explicitly make a request (like a `MapboxNavigation#requestRoutes` request for a route).

### Monthly active user (MAU)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#monthly-active-user-mau "Direct link to Monthly active user (MAU)")

Here's a breakdown of how the Navigation SDK counts monthly active users.

#### How is the _monthly_ period determined?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#how-is-the-monthly-period-determined "Direct link to how-is-the-monthly-period-determined")

The month-long period aligns with your billing period, which is usually aligned with the calendar month. To see the billing period for your account, visit the [Invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
. Even if you have a shortened first billing period, your MAU statistics will start on the first day and end on the last day of each month.

#### When is a user considered _active_?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#when-is-a-user-considered-active "Direct link to when-is-a-user-considered-active")

A user of your application will be counted as a monthly active user as soon as they start at least one Free Drive or Active Guidance trip. To learn more about trips, see the [Trip](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#trip)
 section of this guide.

#### How are unique _users_ counted?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#how-are-unique-users-counted "Direct link to how-are-unique-users-counted")

A single user is billed as one MAU across app upgrades as long as the app is not deleted. Deleting and re-installing an app that uses the Navigation SDK would result in an additional MAU. Additionally, each simulated device used in development and testing will count as an MAU. As the SDK does not store personally identifying information, Mapbox cannot exclude individual devices from billing. Instead, a generous free tier for MAUs allows for a set number of devices to use the SDK without charge.

Pricing options[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#pricing-options "Direct link to Pricing options")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Read about the two available pricing options and learn which line items are associated with which types of usage. Then you can visit the pricing page to see the cost per line item and use our pricing calculators to estimate your bills.

### SDKs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#sdks "Direct link to SDKs")

#### Navigation SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#navigation-sdk "Direct link to Navigation SDK")

In case of a Navigation SDK usage, Maps-related MAUs and integrated Search SDK functionality are always billed separately from the navigation usage.

A user who meets the criteria of an _active user_ of **both** the **Navigation SDK** (see [When is a user considered _active_?](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#when-is-a-user-considered-active)
 above) and the **Maps SDK** (see the [Maps SDK pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#when-is-a-user-considered-active)
) will be billed as _both_ Navigation SDK and Maps SDK MAUs. For example, if you initialize the Navigation SDK, display a map to the user, the user selects a destination on the map, and starts a navigation session to that destination, then that user would be billed as both a "Maps SDKs for Mobile" MAU and "Navigation SDK for Mobile" MAU.

A user who meets the criteria of an _active user_ of **only the Navigation SDK** will be billed only as a Navigation-related MAU. For example, if you initialize the Navigation SDK, a user inputs a coordinate to navigate to in a text field, and starts a navigation session, then that user would be billed only as a "Navigation SDK for Mobile" MAU.

A user who meets the criteria of an _active user_ of **only the Maps SDK** will be billed only as a Maps-related MAU even if you are using the Maps SDK via the Navigation SDK in your application. For example, if you initialize the Navigation SDK, display a map to a user, and preview a route without starting a navigation session, you would be billed for that user as a "Maps SDKs for Mobile" MAU.

Visit the pricing page to see the cost per ["Maps SDKs for Mobile"](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#mapsmaus)
, ["Search SDK requests"](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#search)
, and "Navigation SDK for Mobile" (cost varies by [pricing option](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#pricing-options)
).

### Option 1: Metered trips pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#option-1-metered-trips-pricing "Direct link to Option 1: Metered trips pricing")

Metered trips pricing can be more cost-efficient for applications with any number of monthly users but whose users complete relatively few trips during a billing period. When you enable metered trips pricing on your account, the system bills you for two types of usage: trips and monthly active users (MAUs).

**Trips**: How trips are defined, the cost per trip, and the line item that will appear on your invoice varies by trip type as defined in the [Trip](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#trip)
 section above. Depending on what trip types are used in your application, this usage will be labeled on any [invoices](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 (if you exceed the free tier) and on your [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
 as:

*   "Free Drive Trip for Mobile"
*   "Active Guidance Trip for Mobile"

**Monthly active users**: You will also be charged a fee for each user of your application in a month-long period. The cost per monthly user for this option is significantly less than the cost per monthly user in [Option 2: Unlimited trips pricing](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#option-2-unlimited-trips-pricing)
. This will be labeled as "Navigation SDK for Mobile" on any [invoices](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 (if you exceed the free tier) and on your [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
.

To see the costs per trip and MAU, visit the ["Navigation SDK - Metered Trips" section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#nav2sesmau)
.

#### Trip Grace Period[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#trip-grace-period "Direct link to Trip Grace Period")

When the Navigation SDK v2.4.0 or later is used, each starting trip session has a grace period of 30 seconds. This means that a session will be counted only after this period has surpassed. This allows you to reduce the cost of using the SDK during development and testing of your applications, as well as in production. The grace period is especially helpful in decreasing the cost of short Free Drive sessions that are a transition between Active Guidance sessions, or when a session is aborted at once after it was started.

#### Example metered trips invoice[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#example-metered-trips-invoice "Direct link to Example metered trips invoice")

For example, if throughout May you had 100 users who each navigated for 6 hours in free drive mode and 300 users who each navigated 4 one-leg routes in active guidance mode, then your invoice would include:

*   Cost per "Free Drive Trips for Mobile" x 600 = $0.00
*   Cost per "Active Guidance Trips for Mobile" x 1200 = $16.00
*   Cost per "Navigation SDK for Mobile" x 400 = $90.00

Your total cost for navigation that month would be $106.00. You can estimate your monthly costs using the calculators on our [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#nav2fdtrip)
.

#### Optimize metered trips costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#optimize-metered-trips-costs "Direct link to Optimize metered trips costs")

##### Retain session related types[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#retain-session-related-types "Direct link to Retain session related types")

Destroying an instance of `MapboxNavigation` will stop the corresponding trip session. Recreating the object and continuing the same logical trip will result in multiple sessions billed. Depending on how your app is structured, you might want to retain the `MapboxNavigation` while users open other parts of your application.

##### Pausing trips[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#pausing-trips "Direct link to Pausing trips")

While `MapboxNavigation#startTripSession` start the Free Drive or Active Guidance (if there's a route set) billing sessions and initiates the flow of data, the [`MapboxNavigation#stopTripSession`](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1/navigation/com.mapbox.navigation.core/-mapbox-navigation/stop-trip-session.html)
 only stops the flow of data and pauses the running session.

This means that if the session type does not change (for example, there's no route added/removed), or the maximum time of 12 hours does not elapse, you can extend the validity of the session when you don't need the data and resume that same session by calling `MapboxNavigation#startTripSession` again.

### Option 2: Unlimited trips pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#option-2-unlimited-trips-pricing "Direct link to Option 2: Unlimited trips pricing")

Unlimited trips pricing can be more cost-efficient for applications with any number of monthly users who each complete many trips during a billing period. When you set your account to unlimited trips pricing, you will only see one type of usage: "Navigation SDK for Mobile" MAUs. This means your monthly charges can be more predictable if you know the number of users you have.

To see the number of Navigation SDKs MAUs included in the free tier and the cost per Navigation SDKs MAU beyond the free tier, visit the ["Navigation SDK - Unlimited Trips" section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#navmaus)
.

#### Example unlimited trips invoice[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#example-unlimited-trips-invoice "Direct link to Example unlimited trips invoice")

For example, if you have 200 delivery drivers using your application to take 50 trips per month, then your invoice would include only:

*   Cost per "Navigation SDK for Mobile" x 200 = $950.00

Your total cost for navigation that month would be $950.00. You can estimate your monthly costs using the calculators on our pricing page, starting with [Navigation SDKs](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#navmaus)
.

#### Optimize unlimited trips costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#optimize-unlimited-trips-costs "Direct link to Optimize unlimited trips costs")

Start a [Free Drive](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/get-started/initialization/#start-a-trip-session)
 session before making any resource requests. In this case, these requests will be [covered by Navigation SDK MAU](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#which-mapbox-services-are-included)
.

Legacy pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/#legacy-pricing "Direct link to Legacy pricing")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For pricing information on pre Navigation SDK, see:

*   **v2.0.0-rc.1 and later v2 versions**: [Navigation SDK v2 pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/pricing/)
    
*   **v1.0.0 to v2.0.0-beta.25**: [Legacy pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/)
    
*   **Before v1.0.0**: [Legacy pricing page](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#navigation-sdk-for-ios-and-android)
    

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Navigation SDK v2 | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK for Android v2
=============================

Current version:`v2.20.3`

*   check
    
    Navigation UI components ready to integrate into your application
    
*   check
    
    Professionally designed map styles
    
*   check
    
    Worldwide driving, cycling, and walking directions
    
*   check
    
    Traffic avoidance and proactive rerouting
    

A newer version of the Navigation SDK is available

This page uses v2.20.3 of the Mapbox Navigation SDK. A newer version of the SDK is available. Learn about the latest version, v3.11.0-beta.1, in the [Navigation SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/)
.

The Navigation SDK for Android allows you to build a complete navigation experience with the power of the [Mapbox Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/#directions)
. You can build a complete turn-by-turn navigation UI inside your application using our pre-built components or use the core logic directly to build something truly custom.

The Navigation SDK provides a collection of features that are critical when building navigation projects, including:

*   Navigation routes
*   Accurate device location updates
*   Voice instruction announcements
*   Real-time user progress to their destination
*   Off-route detection and rerouting
*   Dynamic camera adjustments during turn-by-turn navigation
*   Pre-built UI components to show changing navigation information
*   Support for navigating with limited or no network connection

Types of navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#types-of-navigation "Direct link to Types of navigation")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK offers two types of navigation: turn-by-turn navigation and free-drive mode.

### Turn-by-turn[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#turn-by-turn "Direct link to Turn-by-turn")

Turn-by-turn navigation, also referred to as active guidance, is likely what comes to mind when you think about a typical turn-by-turn navigation experience: a user receives turn-by-turn instructions as they progress along the route. Read more in the [Turn-by-turn navigation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/turn-by-turn-navigation/)
 guides.

### Free-drive[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#free-drive "Direct link to Free-drive")

Free-drive navigation, also referred to as passive navigation, is a unique Mapbox Navigation SDK feature that allows drivers to take advantage of some navigation features without selecting a route and following turn-by-turn instructions to a set destination. Read more in the [Free-drive](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/free-drive/)
 guide.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android is compatible with applications that:

*   Are built using the Android SDK 21 or higher.
*   Are built using the NDK 21. Other major versions of the NDK are not guaranteed to be compatible.
*   Use Java 8 for `sourceCompatibility` and `targetCompatibility`, as shown in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/get-started/install/#add-the-dependency)
    .

Java compatibility[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#java-compatibility "Direct link to Java compatibility")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android is built with Kotlin, the official language recommended by Google for Android development. Kotlin is 100% interoperable with Java.

Applications with a Java codebase can interact properly with the public APIs exposed by Mapbox SDKs for Android. If you experience any issues using the Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android with Java, [contact us](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us)
.

Use with other Mapbox tools[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#use-with-other-mapbox-tools "Direct link to Use with other Mapbox tools")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android is designed to be used with several other Mapbox products.

### Directions API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#directions-api "Direct link to Directions API")

The Navigation SDK comes with access to the Mapbox Directions API to generate routes between two or more coordinates. The Directions API has many route options like setting a `profile` (for example driving, driving with traffic, or walking), `bearing` (to influence the direction in which a route will begin), and `exclude` (for example, avoiding tolls). Read more about the Directions API in the [API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/)
.

### Maps SDK for Android[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#maps-sdk-for-android "Direct link to Maps SDK for Android")

By default, the Navigation SDK comes with access to the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android. The Navigation SDK uses the Maps SDK to display routes on a map and show the user's location on the map throughout their trip to provide both navigation-related context and nearby places that may be of interest to the driver. The Navigation SDK uses Mapbox Maps SDK v10, you can find documentation for your version in the [Maps SDK API references listing](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/api-reference/)
.

Conditions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#conditions "Direct link to Conditions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#attribution "Direct link to Attribution")

If you use a Mapbox map in your application, you are required to provide attribution according to the [Mapbox Maps SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#attribution)
.

### Terms[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#terms "Direct link to Terms")

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is distributed under [Mapbox's Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos)
.

### Pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#pricing "Direct link to Pricing")

Read more about pricing for the Navigation SDK in the [pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/pricing/)
.

Older versions of the SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/v2/guides/#older-versions-of-the-sdk "Direct link to Older versions of the SDK")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Looking for an older version of the Navigation SDK? See the [Navigation SDK v1 documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/legacy/navigation/guides/)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Search SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search SDK for Android
======================

Current version:`v2.14.0-alpha.2`[View changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-search-android/releases)

*   check
    
    Add pre-built UI components to your app
    
*   check
    
    Search for places by name, category, or coordinate
    
*   check
    
    Search for addresses offline
    

[Install](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/install/)
[githubContribute on GitHub](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-search-android)

The **Mapbox Search SDK for Android** gives you the tools you need to add a search experience to your application. The Search SDK consists of various components that can be used standalone or combined together so you can build something truly custom. It takes only a few minutes to drop a pre-built user interface into your application. Let's learn about the components more!

There are two lines of development: versions 1.x and 2.x. They only differ in the runtime dependencies they have.

Versions 1.x depend on Maps Version 10. Versions 2.x depend on Maps Version 11.

Address Autofill[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#address-autofill "Direct link to Address Autofill")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Suppose you need a user to enter a valid home or delivery address. They typically need to do this during the registration or checkout process, where making the entry process as smooth as possible can help you prevent churn. This is where the [Address Autofill](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/address-autofill/)
 will help you to enable users to fill in the address form by entering only a few characters is the most beneficial.

  

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/assets/medias/search-autofill-demo-257a6197a64ee4750643d7a1f14cb429.mp4)
.

Category Search[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#category-search "Direct link to Category Search")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Category search](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/search-by-category/)
 for Android gives you the tools you need to add POI (Points of Interest) search to your application. Whether your users need to search for cafes nearby, parking in another city, or Electric Vehicle (EV) charging stations along their route, this use case will help you to provide the functionality they need with a few lines of code.

  

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/assets/medias/search-discover-demo-681c1d1692801a77fd271c23a3be6c53.mp4)
.

Place Autocomplete[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#place-autocomplete "Direct link to Place Autocomplete")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Place Autocomplete](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/search-by-text/)
 returns place suggestions such as addresses, businesses and POIs in response to user text search queries, as well as by supplying [numeric coordinate information](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/search-by-coordinate/)
.

  

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/assets/medias/search-place-autocomplete-demo-a6993f677b4dd90deac25a47aae6a4b9.mp4)
.

Search Engine and Offline Search Engine[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#search-engine-and-offline-search-engine "Direct link to Search Engine and Offline Search Engine")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

None of the use cases above covers your needs? Use generic [Search Engine](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/search-engine/)
 that allows you to build something custom. Similarly, you can use [Offline Search Engine](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/search-engine/offline/)
 to add offline search experience to your applications.

  

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/assets/medias/search-online-offline-maps-demo-8bfecab58618d8abd9ae96f73d3eed8f.mp4)
.

Search UI[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#search-ui "Direct link to Search UI")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [Search UI](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/search-ui/)
 is built on top of the core functionality discussed above and provides ready-made search UI elements that you can use to add a search experience to your app. You can adjust the style of various elements of the preset UI and customize results.

  

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/assets/medias/search-results-view-dark-demo-04bac2c62ebbebe4ca11e5ce2fe06cc4.mp4)
.

Why use SDK?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#why-use-sdk "Direct link to Why use SDK?")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If your primary use case is targeted to mobile devices (iOS & Android), we strongly encourage you to use our Search SDK. With Search SDK, you get:

### Stable SDK API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#stable-sdk-api "Direct link to Stable SDK API")

By defining our use cases, we aim to support the same interface for longer even if the underlying implementation changes. This means bumping the SDK version fixes all issues you might encounter without requiring you to rework the app or its integration code.

### Fewer lines of code[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#fewer-lines-of-code "Direct link to Fewer lines of code")

Developers can get up and running with a few lines of code to start the SDK. See for yourself, it’s readable, maintainable, and error-proof.

### Protecting against errors and pitfalls[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#protecting-against-errors-and-pitfalls "Direct link to Protecting against errors and pitfalls")

Having a ready-made integration example enables you to experiment faster and avoid common pitfalls of using API in a non-preferred way.

### Drop-in UI support[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#drop-in-ui-support "Direct link to Drop-in UI support")

Have no need for custom UI and ready to get started? We have you covered with an easy-to-blend UI that doesn’t require extensive binding to current components and navigation flow.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Search SDK for Android works with:

*   Android 5.0+ (API 21)
*   NDK 23 or NDK 27. Other major versions of the NDK are not guaranteed to be compatible
*   Architectures: armeabi-v7a, arm64-v8a, x86, x86\_64
*   Kotlin 1.7 or newer

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Maps SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Maps SDK for Android
====================

Current version:`v11.13.2`[View changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-android/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    Map styles
    
*   check
    
    Runtime styling
    
*   check
    
    Camera manipulation
    
*   check
    
    Querying the map
    
*   check
    
    User interaction
    

[Install](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/install/)
[githubContribute on GitHub](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-android/)

The **Mapbox Maps SDK for Android** is a library for embedding highly customized maps within Android applications.

**Select a style for your application.** Start with the expertly designed [Mapbox Standard](https://docs.mapbox.com/map-styles/standard/)
, Mapbox Standard Satellite or any other [Mapbox-designed styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/styles/#mapbox-styles)
 that are optimized for various use cases, or design your own custom style in [Mapbox Studio](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-studio)
. You can even alter your map's style at runtime.

**Add your own custom data to the map.** Whether you're adding markers to a map to include a store locator right in your app or creating compelling data visualizations, the Maps SDK gives you control over how you display data on your map. You can style map features at runtime based on data properties, user interaction, or device settings.

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/assets/medias/maps-example-circle-layer-clustering-activity-b51d674dc3304554c065da58cf3e4e48.mp4)
.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Maps SDK for Android requirements are:

*   Apps must be built using Android SDK 21 or higher.
*   Apps that use the NDK must be built using NDK 23. Other major versions of the NDK are not guaranteed to be compatible.
*   Devices that support OpenGL ES 3.
*   Apps developed with Kotlin, must use version 1.6.0 or later.
*   Use Java 8 or later for `sourceCompatibility` and `targetCompatibility`.

Java compatibility[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#java-compatibility "Direct link to Java compatibility")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Maps SDK for Android is built with Kotlin, the official language recommended by Google for Android development. Kotlin is 100% interoperable with Java.

Applications with a Java codebase can interact properly with the public APIs exposed by Mapbox SDKs for Android. If you experience any issues using the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android with Java, [contact us](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us)
.

Use with other Mapbox tools[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#use-with-other-mapbox-tools "Direct link to Use with other Mapbox tools")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android with other Mapbox products. Mix and match other tools and plugins to create a location-savvy application to your exact specifications.

### Static maps[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#static-maps "Direct link to Static maps")

The Mapbox Maps SDK for Android includes methods to generate a static snapshot of an existing map within your application. Use the `MapSnapshotter` class to create a static raster image of a map, without having to make a request to another API.

The [Java SDK](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/java/guides/services/static-image/)
 is also available for applications that need direct access to the [Mapbox Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/)
.

### Mapbox Studio[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#mapbox-studio "Direct link to Mapbox Studio")

The Mapbox Maps SDK is compatible with [Mapbox Studio](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-studio)
. Design a map that seamlessly matches your brand and application design, then enhance it with your own custom data all within the Mapbox Studio web interface.

Conditions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#conditions "Direct link to Conditions")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#attribution "Direct link to Attribution")

You must include the Mapbox wordmark on any map that uses the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android. You must display attribution on maps unless the map's data comes entirely from non-Mapbox sources.

You may adjust the position of the Mapbox wordmark and attribution notice, but they must stay visible on the map. You may also change the background and text color of the attribution notice to match your design aesthetics, but all information must be legible.

You may not otherwise alter the Mapbox wordmark or text attribution notice.

[GUIDE\
\
Attribution guidelines\
\
Learn more about what kinds of attribution Mapbox requires and why.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/how-mapbox-works/attribution/)

### Telemetry opt out[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#telemetry-opt-out "Direct link to Telemetry opt out")

Mapbox Telemetry is a [powerful location analytics platform](https://www.mapbox.com/telemetry/)
 included in this SDK. By default, the SDK sends unidentified location and usage data to Mapbox whenever the host app causes it to be gathered. The [Mapbox Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/tos/)
 require your app to provide users with a way to individually opt out of Mapbox Telemetry, which is provided automatically as part of the [attribution](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#attribution)
 control. If you hide the attribution control, you must provide an alternative opt out for your users to use.

Older versions of the SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#older-versions-of-the-sdk "Direct link to Older versions of the SDK")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Maps SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/#maps-sdk "Direct link to Maps SDK")

Looking for an older version of the Maps SDK? See the [Maps SDK v10 API reference](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/api/10.19.0/)
 or the [Maps SDK v9 documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/legacy/maps/guides/)
.

# Navigation | API Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The **Mapbox Navigation Service** includes several APIs for getting directions or building navigation experiences, either by interacting with the Navigation Service APIs directly or using a mobile SDK. This includes services for requesting [directions](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/)
, creating [isochrones](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/isochrone/)
, [matching routes](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/map-matching/)
 to the road network on a map, and more.

Integrate the Navigation Service APIs into mobile apps with the [Mapbox Navigation SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/overview/)
 and [Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/overview/)
.

[Directions API\
\
The Mapbox Directions API calculates optimal routes and produces turn-by-turn instructions.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/)

[Isochrone API\
\
The Mapbox Isochrone API computes areas that are reachable within a specified amount of time from a location.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/isochrone/)

[EV Charge Finder API\
\
BETA\
\
The Mapbox EV Charge Finder API provides a cohesive set of services for EV use cases.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/ev-charge-finder/)

[Map Matching API\
\
The Mapbox Map Matching API snaps fuzzy, inaccurate traces to the road and path network.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/map-matching/)

[Matrix API\
\
The Mapbox Matrix API returns travel times between many points.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/matrix/)

[Optimization API v1\
\
The Mapbox Optimization API returns a duration-optimized route between the input coordinates.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/optimization-v1/)

[Optimization API v2\
\
BETA\
\
The Mapbox Optimization API returns a duration-optimized route between the input coordinates.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/optimization/)

[Using HTTP POST\
\
The Directions API and Map Matching API both support access using the HTTP POST method.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/http-post/)

[Navigation service changelog\
\
This changelog documents all changes made to Navigation service APIs.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/changelog/)

Maps
----

Add custom maps to web and mobile apps.

Maps client libraries & SDKs

### 

Mapbox GL JS

Add custom interactive maps to web applications.

[GL JS Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/)

### 

Mobile SDKs

[androidAndroid SDK Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/)

Data loading & access APIs

Mapbox Tiling Service

Generate and host vector tilesets for use in applications.

[API Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)

[MTS Manual](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/)

### 

Uploads API

Create raster and vector tilesets from spatial data.

[API Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/uploads/)

### 

Datasets API

Manage vector datasets in the cloud for use in tilesets.

[API Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/datasets/)

### 

Tilequery API

Query for vector tileset features near a point.

[API Playground](https://docs.mapbox.com/playground/tilequery/)

Tiling & rendering APIs

### 

Vector Tiles API

Raster Tiles API

Static Images API

Build custom map images.

### 

Static Tiles API

Mapbox Studio

Styles API

Fonts API

Other Docs
----------

### 

Accounts and Pricing

Understand your account settings, billing, and usage.

[Account Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/)

### 

Tokens API

Manage access tokens and scopes programmatically.

[Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/tokens/)

### 

Atlas

Run Mapbox offline or on your own infrastructure.

[Atlas Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/atlas)

### 

Mapbox in Tableau

Create geospatial visualizations in Tableau.

[Tableau Extension Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/geographic-analytics/overview/)

### 

Android Core library

Handle device location, permissions, and connectivity.

[Library Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/core/guides/)

### 

Maps SDK for Unity

Build Unity applications from real map data.

[SDK Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/unity/maps/guides/)

### 

Mapbox MCP Server

Integrate Mapbox location APIs into AI applications.

[MCP Server Docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/guides/mcp-server/)

# API Reference | Navigation SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api-reference/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

With the Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android, you can create a personalized navigation experience using the capabilities of the [Mapbox Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/#directions)
, [Mapbox Map Matching](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/map-matching/)
, and [Mapbox Maps](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/)
.

[v3.11.0-beta.1 latest\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/api/coreframework/3.11.0-beta.1)
