# Post-launch checklist | Help | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/post-launch-checklist/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Post-launch checklist
=====================

A short list of items to review after publishing your map.

[Get started](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/)

![](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/img/dev-journey-map/arrow.svg)

[Add map](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/start-mapping/)

![](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/img/dev-journey-map/arrow.svg)

[Add data](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/data-guide/)

![](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/img/dev-journey-map/arrow.svg)

[Configure interactions](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/configure-interactions/)

![](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/img/dev-journey-map/arrow.svg)

[Deploy](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/post-launch-checklist/)

As you iterate on your map, here are some key resources and actions you might consider.

Update your map's data[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/post-launch-checklist/#update-your-maps-data "Update your map's data への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If the underlying source data of your map changes, there are several ways for you to update your map:

*   **Externally Hosted Data:** If your data is hosted externally and loaded at runtime, update the external source or replace the source link if it has changed.
*   **Studio Uploads:** For data originally uploaded in Studio, open the style and update the layer's source with the new data.
*   **Using APIs directly:** If you used the Uploads API or Mapbox Tiling Service:
    *   Upload a new tileset and update the tileset ID in your Studio style or client-side code.
    *   Replace your existing tileset. See [Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/uploads/#create-an-upload)
         and [MTS](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#replace-a-tileset-source)
         for detailed instructions.

Note

Tileset changes take around 15 minutes to reflect on your maps. Learn more about [API caching](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/api-caching/)
.

Before publishing your changes, inspect your map to verify that no broken references occur due to data changes.

Update your map's style[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/post-launch-checklist/#update-your-maps-style "Update your map's style への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can always update the visual look and feel of your map:

*   **In Studio:** Edit the style directly. Changes are saved as drafts and won't deploy to your production apps until you publish them.
*   **Client-side code:** For custom layers, update the paint properties. For the Mapbox Standard Style, change the basemap's [configuration properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/guides/migrate-to-v3/#explore-new-features)
    .

Note

Style changes take around 15 minutes to reflect on your maps. Learn more about [API caching](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/api-caching/)
.

Update your SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/post-launch-checklist/#update-your-sdk "Update your SDK への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It's vital to keep your SDK up-to-date for optimal performance and security of your web or mobile app, and to take advantage of our latest features. We release new versions of our SDKs approximately every six weeks. You can track these updates and view detailed changelogs for each platform:

*   **Android SDK**: Follow [the changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-android/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
     for Android updates.
*   **iOS SDK**: Follow [the changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-ios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
     for iOS updates.
*   **GL JS**: Stay informed on new [GL JS releases](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/releases)
    .

Monitor your map's usage[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/map-developer-journey/post-launch-checklist/#monitor-your-maps-usage "Monitor your map's usage への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Stay informed about your map's performance and billing:

*   **Statistics:** Visit your account's [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
     for API usage data.
*   **Invoices:** Check your [Invoice page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
     view your upcoming and past invoices.

If you have any questions about your implementation, pricing, or other ways you can work with our platform:

*   Post your question to our [Discord community](https://discord.gg/uMpcC5RmJh)
    
*   View our [support plans](https://www.mapbox.com/support#support-plans)
    
*   Contact our [Sales team](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales)

# bounding box | Help | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/bounding-box/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

A **bounding box** is a way to display a rectangular area of a map. It is expressed as a set of coordinate pairs, with the first coordinate pair referring to the southwestern corner of the box (the minimum [longitude and latitude](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/lat-lon/)
) and the second referring to the northeastern corner of the box (the maximum longitude and latitude).

Below is a visualization of a bounding box created around the state of Vermont from our [Location Helper](https://labs.mapbox.com/location-helper)
 tool.

Bounding boxes are useful for describing the spatial limits of geographic features. For example, a you could create a bounding box to describe the limits of an administrative boundary polygon, or describe the limits of a set of point features.

Once a bounding box is defined, you can use it to set the initial view of a map to make sure that the area within the bounding box is fully visible regardless of aspect ratio. You can also use a bounding box to control the view of an existing map programmatically.

### Mapbox GL JS[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/bounding-box/#mapbox-gl-js "Mapbox GL JS への直接リンク")

This [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/mapbox-gl-js/)
 snippet creates a bounding box using the [`LngLatBounds` method](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/api/geography/#lnglatbounds)
 and then calls [`fitBounds`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/api/map/#map#fitbounds)
 to move the map's camera, ensuring that the bounding box is fully in view.

    const southWest = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9876, 40.7661);const northEast = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9397, 40.8002);const boundingBox = new mapboxgl.LngLatBounds(southWest, northEast);map.fitBounds(boundingBox);

[EXAMPLE\
\
Fit map to a bounding box\
\
This GL JS example shows you how to use `fitBounds` to zoom & pan to a bounding box.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/example/fitbounds/)
[EXAMPLE\
\
Initialize a map with a bounding box\
\
This GL JS example demonstrates how a bounding box can be used to center a map regardless of the map's aspect ratio.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/example/initialize-with-bounding-box/)

### Mobile SDKs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/bounding-box/#mobile-sdks "Mobile SDKs への直接リンク")

Our mobile SDK's for [iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/)
, [Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/guides/)
 & [Flutter](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/flutter/maps/guides/)
 use the `CoordinateBounds` class to create a bounding box and use the `camera` and `setCamera` methods of the `mapboxMap` object to set the camera view to the bounding box.

iOS

Android

Flutter

iOS

Android

Flutter

    let boundingBox = CoordinateBounds(southwest: CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: -0.90204, longitude: -158.63249),northeast: CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: 66.77350, longitude: 10.68889))// Center the camera on the boundslet camera = mapView.mapboxMap.camera(for: boundingBox, padding: .zero, bearing: 0, pitch: 0)

iOS

Android

Flutter

    let boundingBox = CoordinateBounds(Point.fromLngLat(-158.63249 -0.90204),Point.fromLngLat(10.68889, 66.77350),false)// Define camera boundsprivate val cameraBoundsOptions = CameraBoundsOptions.Builder().bounds(boundingBox).minZoom(10.0).build()// Fit camera to the bounding boxmapboxMap.setCamera(cameraBoundsOptions)

iOS

Android

Flutter

    let boundingBox = CoordinateBounds(southwest: Point( coordinates: Position(42.73834, -73.45338)),northeast: Point( coordinates: Position(45.02491,  -71.48691)),infiniteBounds: false)// Center the camera on the bounding boxlet camera = mapboxMap.cameraForCoordinateBounds(boundingBox);mapboxMap.setCamera(camera);

Note

A bounding box's edges are not explicitly parallel on all map [projections](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/guides/projections/)
 as they follow longitude & latitude. In this image you can see that the bounding box edges curve to follow longitude/latitude in the [`globe`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/guides/projections/#globe)
 projection.

**Related resources:**

Any Mapbox library that creates maps or initiates geocoding requests has a class or object for using bounding boxes in your code:

*   [Mapbox Geocoding API `bbox` parameter documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/search/geocoding/#forward-geocoding-with-search-text-input)
    
*   [Mapbox GL JS `LngLatBounds` documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/api/geography/#lnglatbounds)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS `CoordinateBounds` documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/11.13.0/documentation/mapboxmaps/coordinatebounds/)
    
*   [Mapbox Maps SDK for Android `CoordinateBounds` class documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/api/11.13.0/mapbox-maps-android/com.mapbox.maps/-coordinate-bounds/)
    
*   [Mapbox Java SDK `MapboxGeocoding.Builder.bbox()` method documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/java/api/libjava-services/7.0.0/com/mapbox/api/geocoding/v5/MapboxGeocoding.Builder.html#bbox-java.lang.String-)
    
*   [Mapbox Search SDK for iOS `BoundingBox` documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/search/api/core/2.9.0-rc.2/Structs/BoundingBox.html)
    
*   [Turf.js `turf.bbox` documentation](https://turfjs.org/docs/api/bbox)
    
*   [Location helper - interact with a map and get bounding box values to use in your code.](https://labs.mapbox.com/location-helper)

# Get Started with Maps SDK for iOS | Maps SDK | iOS | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This guide will walk you through installing the most recent version of the **Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS**, configuring your iOS app to use the Maps SDK, and initializing a Mapbox map in your iOS app.

Prerequisites[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#prerequisites "Prerequisites への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **A Mapbox account**: Sign up or log into a free account on [Mapbox](https://account.mapbox.com/auth/signup/)
    .
*   **Xcode**: Install Xcode to your device through the Macbook App Store.

Part 1: Configure your credentials[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#part-1-create-and-configure-your-credentials "Part 1: Configure your credentials への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before starting to develop your application with the Maps SDK, you'll need to create and configure your credentials.

### Step 1: Log in/Sign up for a Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#step-1-login-sign-up "Step 1: Log in/Sign up for a Mapbox account への直接リンク")

Login to your [Mapbox account](https://console.mapbox.com/)
. If you don't have an account, you can [sign up for free](https://account.mapbox.com/auth/signup/)
.

Your account includes a default public access token and allows you to create a secret access token for use in the installation of the SDK.

### Step 2: Configure your public token[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#step-2-configure-your-public-token "Step 2: Configure your public token への直接リンク")

Follow these steps to grab a public access token from your Mapbox account and add it to your project's `Info.plist`.

1.  Open your project or create a new project in Xcode.
    *   If creating a new project, we recommend using the `App` project type.
2.  Navigate to your project's `Info.plist` file.
    *   This is located in your main project page. You can go here by double clicking the project title in the top left corner, then `Info` in the middle of the screen.
3.  Hover over a key and click the plus button.
4.  Type `MBXAccessToken` into the key field and hit tab to select the value field.
5.  Grab your default public access token.
    *   To grab your token, open up a browser and go to your account's [tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
        .
    *   On the top of your token list, copy your **Default Public Token**.
6.  Go back to Xcode and in the value field, paste in your default public token.

Your public access token is now available for use in your iOS project and you will be able to access the Mapbox SDK.

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/assets/medias/MapsInstallPublicToken-eb2f6fbf8715def5ae8eaa35a5b562c4.mp4)
.

**Advanced Topics: Best Practices, Rotating Tokens & Adding Tokens at Runtime**

[GUIDE\
\
Access token best practices\
\
Learn how to keep access tokens private in mobile apps.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/private-access-token-android-and-ios/)

**Adding Tokens at Runtime**

You can also implement tokens at runtime, but this requires you to have a separate server to store your tokens. This is helpful if you want to rotate your tokens or add additional security by storing your tokens outside of the application, but is a much more complex method of implementation.

If you do choose to follow this method, we recommend calling `MapboxOptions.accessToken = YOUR_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN` before inflating the `MapView`, to avoid errors from occurring.

**Rotating Tokens**

For more information on access token rotation, consult the [Access Tokens Information page](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/how-mapbox-works/access-tokens/)
.

Part 2: Add the dependency[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#part-2-add-the-dependency "Part 2: Add the dependency への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mapbox provides the Maps SDK via **Swift Package Manager**, **CocoaPods** and **direct download**. You can choose whichever you prefer.

Swift Package Manager

CocoaPods

Direct download

Follow the steps below to download and install the Mapbox Maps SDK via the Swift Package Manager.

1.  In your Xcode project or workspace, go to **File** > **Add Packages Dependencies...**.
2.  Enter the following GitHub URL into the search bar in the top right corner:

    https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-ios.git

3.  Select the _Dependency Rule_ you want to apply to the Maps SDK
    *   We recommend selecting "Up to Next Major Version" for the _Dependency Rule_, specifying `11.0.0` as the minimum version. This allows you to select the most recently released stable minor version of the SDK.
    *   To instead install a specific version set the _Dependency Rule_ field to "Exact Version" and insert the desired version. The latest stable version of the Maps SDK is `11.13.2`.
4.  Hit enter or Click **Add Package**.
5.  In the new window, click the dropdown under `Add to Target` and select your project target.
6.  Click **Add Package**.
7.  Once the SDK finishes installing, make sure you can see 4 new dependencies on the left side of the editor, under **Package Dependencies**:
    *   `MapboxCommon`
    *   `MapboxCoreMaps`
    *   `MapboxMaps`
    *   `Turf`
8.  Next, click on your project's target, scroll down to `Frameworks, Libraries, and Embedded Content` and make sure `MapboxMaps` is included.
    *   To access your project's target, click the name of your project on the left side of your editor, then on the left side of the inner window, selecting the first name underneath the **Target** header.
    *   If the library is not added, click the `+` button and from the list that appears, select `MapboxMaps` and click **Add**.

Now you can use start using Mapbox in your project by adding `import MapboxMaps` to the top of any of your files.

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/assets/medias/MapsInstallSpm-7efae6e51e075fecd2c355853e1b73e7.mp4)
.

Updating your packages

If you are using the Swift Package Manager and need to update your packages select **File** > **Packages** > **Update To Latest Package Versions**.

Part 3: Add a map[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#part-3-add-a-map "Part 3: Add a map への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can now add a map to your application using either UIKit or SwiftUI, by following these steps:

1.  On the left side of the editor, select the folder with your project name.
2.  Click on the file name `YOURPROJECTNAMEApp`.
3.  Copy the code snippet below and add it to file.
4.  Run your application and you should see an interactable map appear in the simulator.
    *   When running the simulator, make sure to set your build platform to a type of iOS device, for example `iPhone 16`. If you see `My Mac` or `Any iOS Device` at the top of Xcode, switch the type to a specific iOS device.

SwiftUI

UIKit

SwiftUI

UIKit

    import SwiftUIimport MapboxMapsstruct ContentView: View {    var body: some View {        let center = CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: 39.5, longitude: -98.0)        Map(initialViewport: .camera(center: center, zoom: 2, bearing: 0, pitch: 0))            .ignoresSafeArea()    }}

SwiftUI

UIKit

    import UIKitimport MapboxMapsclass ViewController: UIViewController {    override func viewDidLoad() {        super.viewDidLoad()        let mapView = MapView(frame: view.bounds)        let cameraOptions = CameraOptions(center:            CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: 39.5, longitude: -98.0),            zoom: 2, bearing: 0, pitch: 0)        mapView.mapboxMap.setCamera(to: cameraOptions)        mapView.autoresizingMask = [.flexibleWidth, .flexibleHeight]        view.addSubview(mapView)    }}

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#troubleshooting "Troubleshooting への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   If the `MapboxMaps` library is not accessible from your code, try the following:
    *   Make sure the 4 libraries are listed in Frameworks, Libraries, and Embedded Content.
        *   You should see the 4 libraries: `MapboxCommon`, `MapboxCoreMaps`, `MapboxMaps`,`Turf`.
        *   If these are missing, click the plus button and add each of them from the list.
    *   Check that `Embed` dropdown for each library is assigned `Embed & Sign` in the Frameworks, Libraries, and Embedded Content section.
    *   Remove and re-add package if the information above is true.
*   If you cannot run the map in your simulator, try the following:
    *   Make sure your token is added to the Info.plist and is a valid token.
    *   Check that build platform is assigned to a specific iOS device, such as `iPhone 16`. If you see `My Mac` or `Any iOS Device`, this may cause issues when trying to run the simulator.

[GUIDE\
\
Troubleshooting Mapbox Maps and Navigation SDKs for iOS installation\
\
This guide provides more information for troubleshooting installation problems with the Maps SDK.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/)

Next Steps[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#next-steps "Next Steps への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

With the Maps SDK installed and a minimal map rendering in your app, you can explore additional features of the SDK:

*   [Access the user's location](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/user-location/)
    
*   [Understand Markers and Map Annotations](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/markers-and-annotations/)
    
*   [Learn more about the camera and map animations](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/camera-and-animation/)
    

You can also explore example code and tutorials:

[TUTORIAL\
\
Learn how to clone and run the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS examples app.\
\
Learn how to clone and run the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS examples app to see any iOS example in real time.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/maps-sdk-ios-examples-app/)
[EXAMPLE\
\
Add a marker to the map using Point Annotations\
\
Learn how to add a marker to a point of interest on a map by using point annotations on a symbol layer.](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/examples/point-annotation/)
[EXAMPLE\
\
Add a pop-up to the map using View Annotations\
\
Learn how to add a pop-up to a point of interest on a map by using view annotations.](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/examples/view-annotation-basic/)

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# MapView  Reference

[MapView\
=======](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/MapView)

*   `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapView) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/MapView.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/MapInitOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptionsProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(pl)MapInitOptionsProvider) `
    
    A protocol used to provide `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/MapInitOptions.html) ` when initializing a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/MapView.html) ` with a Storyboard or a nib.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Protocols/MapInitOptionsProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/ResourceOptions)

*   `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ResourceOptionsV) `
    
    Options to configure access to a resource
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Structs/ResourceOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Extensions/MapOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlyphsRasterizationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMGlyphsRasterizationOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Extensions/GlyphsRasterizationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[InterfaceOrientationProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28InterfaceOrientationProviderP) `
    
    A protocol that supplies current interface orientation for the specified view.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target (e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Protocols/InterfaceOrientationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionURLOpener](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps20AttributionURLOpenerP) `
    
    A protocol to open attribution URLs.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target(e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Protocols/AttributionURLOpener.html)
    
    #### Declaration

---

[MapView\

*   `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapView) `

*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Classes/MapView.html) `.

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Protocols/MapInitOptionsProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/ResourceOptions)

*   `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ResourceOptionsV) `
    
    Options to configure access to a resource
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Structs/ResourceOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Extensions/MapOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlyphsRasterizationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMGlyphsRasterizationOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Extensions/GlyphsRasterizationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[InterfaceOrientationProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28InterfaceOrientationProviderP) `
    
    A protocol that supplies current interface orientation for the specified view.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target (e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Protocols/InterfaceOrientationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionURLOpener](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps20AttributionURLOpenerP) `
    
    A protocol to open attribution URLs.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target(e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Protocols/AttributionURLOpener.html)
    
    #### Declaration

*   `[AttributionURLOpener](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.1/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps20AttributionURLOpenerP) `

    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target(e.g. application extension target).

# MapView  Reference

[MapView\
=======](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/MapView)

*   `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapView) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapInitOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptionsProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(pl)MapInitOptionsProvider) `
    
    A protocol used to provide `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapInitOptions.html) ` when initializing a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html) ` with a Storyboard or a nib.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Protocols/MapInitOptionsProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/ResourceOptions)

*   `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ResourceOptionsV) `
    
    Options to configure access to a resource
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Extensions/MapOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlyphsRasterizationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMGlyphsRasterizationOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Extensions/GlyphsRasterizationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[InterfaceOrientationProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28InterfaceOrientationProviderP) `
    
    A protocol that supplies current interface orientation for the specified view.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target (e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Protocols/InterfaceOrientationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
